text.title: OpenAI Fine-Tuning Data Preparation — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `fine-tuning-openai-data-prep` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Every line MUST be valid JSON with a top-level 'messages' array (chat fine-tune) or 'prompt'/'completion' (legacy completion).
rule.rationale: OpenAI rejects malformed lines or wrong-schema lines at upload — partial uploads cost no credits but waste cycle time.

rule.statement: Count tokens per example; reject examples exceeding model context (8k/16k/128k depending on model).
rule.rationale: Over-context examples are truncated silently, corrupting training signal.

rule.statement: 80/20 train/val default; use 70/30 when data <500; never train on validation.
rule.rationale: Without a held-out validation set the fine-tune cannot be eval-gated.

rule.statement: For classification tasks, balance classes within 1.5x ratio; oversample or downsample as needed.
rule.rationale: Class imbalance drives the fine-tune toward the majority class.

rule.statement: Use a single canonical system prompt across all training examples; never vary per-example.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent system prompts teach the model the system message is variable, hurting downstream prompting.

rule.statement: Strip / hash PII (emails, phone numbers, IDs) before upload.
rule.rationale: OpenAI fine-tunes are stored on Azure infrastructure; PII leakage is a GDPR breach.
